message:
fix: safety improvements across bulkhead, mysql, grpc, raptorq, cancel, sse, trace

Bulkhead (memory ordering):
- Upgrade pending_queue_count operations from Relaxed to
  Acquire/Release ordering — Relaxed loads could observe stale queue
  state, allowing try_acquire() to barge past queued waiters in a
  TOCTOU window between enqueue's push and counter increment

MySQL (panic elimination + bounds checking):
- PacketBuffer::build_packet() now returns Result instead of
  panicking on oversized payloads; all 8 call sites updated
- Add MAX_COLUMN_COUNT (16384) guard against corrupted/malicious
  servers sending enormous column counts that would cause OOM

gRPC/web:
- Replace unchecked 'as u32' cast with try_from() for frame length,
  preventing silent truncation on >4GB payloads

RaptorQ pipeline:
- Guard against division by zero when symbol_size is 0, returning 0
  source_count instead of panicking in div_ceil

SymbolCancelToken:
- Fix cancellation race: when the loser thread enters the
  already-cancelled branch but finds reason_guard is None (e.g. from
  from_bytes deserialization), set cancelled_at timestamp via CAS so
  downstream code observing is_cancelled() also sees a valid timestamp

SSE:
- Use u128::from(u64::MAX) instead of 'u64::MAX as u128' for the
  min() clamp — semantically identical but avoids the 'as' cast lint

Trace compat:
- Hoist MAX_META_SIZE and MAX_EVENT_SIZE to module-level constants,
  deduplicating the definition across 3 call sites

New test: bulkhead_cancel_bug.rs exercises head-of-line unblocking
when a high-weight queued entry is cancelled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
